contextBuilder.description: Compute vehiclePanel from actor.system.{vehicleIntegrity,overIntegrity}. Both buttons require something to act on: canRollCrit toggles when over-Integrity > 0 OR integrity <= 0 (a vehicle that has just been pushed to zero Integrity gets its first crit roll); canRepair toggles when over-Integrity > 0.

notes: applyTo='both': vehicles in Deathwatch can be player-owned (Battle-Brother gunner / pilot of a kill-team transport) or NPC-owned (hostile gun-wagon, transport). The orchestrator must spread dwVehicleSchemaFields() into BOTH CharacterData and NPCData's defineSchema(), and apply DwVehicleDeclarations on both classes' declare block. The panel gate (isDW) is the same in both sheets — orchestrator must also register the panel include + context build in npc-sheet.ts alongside character-sheet.ts (if npc-sheet doesn't yet host engine panels, this is the first one).

The included template URI uses systems/wh40k-rpg/templates/actor/panel/dw-vehicle-panel.hbs so it matches both player and NPC tab includes — verify the npc sheet's tab structure mirrors the character sheet's tab-status.hbs include site, and add the same {{#if isDW}} block there.

The Repair Test card currently defaults to Challenging difficulty since the DataModel does not persist `lastCritResult`. A follow-up migration that adds `lastCritResult: StringField` to dwVehicleSchemaFields() will let the repair action pick the correct row automatically. The action handler's TODO documents this.

Three additional WH40K.DW.Vehicle.* keys were appended to en.json under the existing namespace (Integrity, OverIntegrity, RollCrit, RollCritTooltip, RepairTest, RepairTestTooltip, RolledLabel, RepairLabel, plus Crit.Header and Repair.Header for the chat-card titles). The i18n-keys.d.ts union was updated inline to match; pnpm i18n:gen will idempotently confirm on next run.

Existing isDW context flag already lands in tab-status.hbs (#162 Cohesion / #164 Renown etc.) — no new system flag is needed for the player sheet. For the NPC sheet, the orchestrator must replicate the gameSystem detection used in character-sheet._prepareContext (isDW = activeGameSystem === 'dw').

The actions module's DwVehicleActionHost interface is a structural duck-type covering both CharacterSheet and NpcSheet's relevant surface (actor + _notify). No applications/* coupling from the actions module.
